reviews.review_text: Loved swimming here in the surf on sunset
Stunning area, really easy to get around and park 😍
Gorgeous beach. Only thing that would make it better would be an off leash option. Great facilities too.
Beautiful beach, nice fine sand, not crowded.

Brunswick Heads is such a beautiful, easygoing place. The river is calm and scenic, perfect for a walk or swim, and the beach is just as lovely. The whole town has a relaxed vibe that makes you want to slow down and stay a bit longer.
Amazing beach. Paradise.
Always clear water and clean sand
Very windy with a marine wind warning in place.
Get there the right time of year & you might just see these guys go by.

A dramatic and expansive white sand beach with few people throughout the week. It will take you a few hours to walk to Byron Bay and you will have to time low tide to traverse the estuary mouth. Don’t forget a hat, sunglasses and sunscreen!

Paid $7.50 (!) for 1.5 hours parking this morning (all parking metered) anticipating a nice long walk on the beach with our small dog. Had looked along the beach the day before and saw an endless expanse of beach but only walked 200m today before encountering a sign saying “no dogs past this point”. Whole experience disappointing. Won’t return. Lovely beach but parking beyond our budget and not worth it for such a short walk.
Really nice beach and a great off leash area for your dogs to exercise with.
Nice local beach. Not much shopping although there were quite a few resale shops if you're interested in those type of things. Not many touristy shops

I love this place.
Always have.
Always will.
Good beach but no amenities like showering place after swimming.
Great beautiful beach. Was easy to swim when we were there but not sure if always the case.
One great river and beach
I Love walking on the beach!
Love this seaside village, such a great atmosphere
Nice little town, disappointed by the parking, we shouldn't have to pay for parking especially if we're going to restaurants and buying from local businesses.
Beautiful chilled out town. My favorite so far. Beautiful crowd- incredible beach everyone is super friendly
We had an enjoyable day riding on the rail trail and exploring the townships along the rail  trail. I hope these communities expand on this through Bryon and beyond .
Amazing beach beautiful, great surf
Place nice very friendly beautiful beach an air bnb was excellent
I left my watch here for 24 hours and it was still there the next day.

Packed solid with people and cars, hard to navigate close to the inlet and bridge, extremely popular.pub was pumping and business flowing with people.
Families enjoyed the open places and water entry.
Get there really really early or it's hopeless looking for parking 😕
This beach is most beautiful 😍
Very busy area
Hard to find parking
Dog Beach right at end. Just bliss
Peace and beauty
Beautiful little town, worth a stop for sure. Friendly wildlife 😉
Wide open spacious beach, only a few people
Pretty and dog friendly just south of the Surf Club.
It was beautiful
Love Brunswick Heads beautiful beach
Nice beach slightly spoiled by the homeless people living behind the beach in the nature reserve
Lots of people and activity in the late afternoon. Nice photo opportunity of the pink sky / beach backdrop.
Very busy..poular
Increíble playa!
Lovely spot with views of Byron and other beaches.
Gorgeous spot, can’t wait to go back
Beautiful to visit and swim. Occational dolphins swim here
Very nice beach with a great amount of sand area.
nice not over crowded
Ahhh the serenity

A great beach to have fun and relax with family.
It's even dog friendly.
A busy day in the carpark but plenty of space on the beach. Surf club had a bbq fundraiser going which was great.
Dogs
The sand squeaks!
Such a beautiful spot. You feel like you are at the beach as if decades ago. Why, because you can't see any buildings from the beach! So lovely!
Great position, surf club carpark good size.

Free drumming circle at Brunswick by the Surf Club every Sunday 4-6pm.
Just come and enjoy the beats. Fire twirlers will likely come out at some stage.  Dance or sit is up to you. A beautiful atmosphere.
Stunning destination very chill and do a jump off the bridge in the cool clear water below if you’re adventurous.
You can't get a parking spot , food is over priced & people are snobbish!
Amazing place
It's one of them Beaches that's Peaceful and Relaxing with an extremely friendly environment.👌😁
Great place for kids, for surfers, for fishing, for latte sipping. Actually, paradise!
Must have been a quiet day as not many people around. Nice beach and good if you hate crowds. Not really any facilities so bring a picnic.

No coffe there 😞😓😩
But maybe my misunderstanding?
Dog walking beach South of the surf lifesaving club as far as the start of the Tyagarah Reserve.
Huge expansive strip of sand which extends all the way south to Byron Bay
Great spot had swim and time at beach
Beautiful beach. Lovely day.
Pretty beach but with so many people squatting in the carpark area makes it hard to get in and out of
Beautiful as always

Nice beach! Not much more to add than that really. Which isn’t to say there’s not more that can be said about it. I’m just saying that as far as beaches go, it’s a nice beach. That’s all. So don’t go all like overthinking it ‘kay? Let’s just move on..
Beautiful
